problem: What triggers cancer cells to leave primary tumor and colonize new tissue?
mechanism: Autophagy activation to clear misfolded proteins
hypothesis: Autophagy activation to clear misfolded proteins may resolve What triggers cancer cells to leave primary tumor and colonize new tissue? by activating at the molecular level.

experiment.protocol: Mouse model knockout study with 36 month follow-up. Control group receives placebo/sham.

wisdom: The impediment to action advances action. What stands in the way becomes the way. — Marcus Aurelius
